title: BI Anomaly Detected Event
description: Emitted when an anomaly is detected in BI analytics metrics.

metadata.description: Event metadata conforming to D2-2 Event Metadata Standard (10-field fixed template).

event_id.description: Globally unique identifier for the event instance.

version.description: Semantic version of the event schema.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the event was produced.

source.description: Origin system or service that produced the event.

producer.description: Specific component or service instance that produced the event.

correlation_id.description: Identifier used to correlate related events across multiple systems.

causation_id.description: Identifier of the event that caused this event (e.g., the parent command).

tenant_id.description: Tenant or organization namespace the event belongs to.

user_id.description: Identifier of the user who triggered the event.

anomaly_id.description: Unique identifier of the detected anomaly.

metric_name.description: Name of the metric that exhibited anomalous behavior.

metric_value.description: Actual observed value of the metric.

expected_value.description: Expected or baseline value of the metric.

deviation_pct.description: Percentage deviation from the expected value.

detected_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the anomaly was detected.

entity_type.description: Type of entity associated with the anomaly (e.g. product, region, channel).

entity_id.description: Identifier of the entity associated with the anomaly.

recommendation.description: Suggested action or recommendation to address the anomaly.

assigned_to.description: User or team assigned to investigate the anomaly.
